sourceEvidence.label: Achilles Tendon Rupture — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Achilles Tendon Rupture

objectives: Identify classic Achilles rupture history and exam.
Explain the Thompson test and resting tension findings.
Compare operative and nonoperative treatment at a high level.

sourceEvidence.label: Achilles Tendon Rupture — Deep study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Deep study template for Achilles Tendon Rupture

anatomy: Tendon blood supply and rupture zone
imaging: Ultrasound or MRI only when exam is equivocal
decisionMaking: Gap, patient activity level, treatment compliance, chronicity
boardPearls: A positive Thompson test in the right story is often enough to act

sourceEvidence.label: Achilles Tendon Rupture — Fast study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Fast study template for Achilles Tendon Rupture

mustKnow: Sudden pop, Thompson test, plantarflexion weakness, early immobilization in equinus
payload.oneLiner: Achilles rupture is usually a clinical diagnosis where early recognition preserves treatment options.
orSurvivalTips: Do not forget comparison to the opposite side

metadata.description: Key physical examination maneuver for achilles tendon rupture.

metadata.claim_text: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for achilles tendon rupture — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.
Achilles rupture is usually a clinical diagnosis where early recognition preserves treatment options.
Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for achilles tendon rupture require attending-defined progression gates.
Positive Thompson test with sudden pop history is often sufficient to diagnose Achilles rupture.
Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating achilles tendon rupture.

metadata.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
